notes: SHIP v1.14.268 - PHASE DOCK (CODE-QUEUE Task 1; owner asked 3x, nothing ships before it). BOTTOM-STACK OPTION SHIPPED: DOCK ABOVE THE LOG BAR, all tightened (owner ruling S44b; the queue requires the note state which option shipped). The five phase cards leave the rack-detail scroll entirely and re-home into a 94vh sheet fronted by a five-chip dock. RE-HOMED, NOT REBUILT: deploy_showRackDetail's own card loop is untouched - an IIFE that SHADOWS `html` captures the cards into a string, so the 73-line loop is byte-identical and there is NO second renderer. Every phase button already re-enters deploy_showRackDetail, so one call at the end of it recomputes the cards and pushes them back into the open sheet - that IS the live-sync (verified: real BLOCK tap inside the sheet -> persisted -> card re-rendered -> dock chip flipped active->blocked -> sheet stayed open). DOCK: five chips (MECH/PWR/NET/COMP/VAL) + a done/total pill, tap -> sheet opens landed on that phase. Bottom offset does NOT hard-assume the LOG bar: #omni-bar hides itself via .omni-inactive when no deploy is active, so the +--omni-h term is :has()-gated on the bar being live, and --omni-h is MEASURED in JS (57px live), never guessed. Measured stack on a 575px viewport: dock 384-434 -> LOG 435-491 -> nav 489-575. No content spacer needed: .page already reserves calc(--tabnav-h + 140px) > the 107px omni+dock stack. SHEET: reuses (A) .va-sheet's scrim + @keyframes sheetUp + initSheetDismiss drag-down, (B) .rd-sheet's body.rd gating, (C) #ta-sheet's body-scroll lock; overscroll-behavior:contain so checklist scroll cannot chain to the page; safe-area both ends; prefers-reduced-motion honored; .phs-inner is ITSELF the scroller (header sticky inside) because initSheetDismiss keys drag-dismiss off inner.scrollTop<=0 - splitting it would let a checklist drag dismiss the sheet. BODY-LEVEL BY NECESSITY: #wk-deploy's .work-sub carries `animation: rf-fade .22s ease both`, i.e. a non-none transform for 220ms, which would make it the containing block for a position:fixed child - the .212 trap in a different organ. TWO REAL BUGS FOUND BY DRIVING THE APP, NOT BY GATES: [1] a bottom-nav tap (showMode) while the sheet was open STRANDED the sheet over the Command Center with body.overflow stuck at hidden - showMode removes ops-detail (which hides the DOCK) but #ph-sheet.open is not gated on ops-detail; fixed by calling phdock_leave() in showMode right beside the existing rd_closeProfile() call, which is there for exactly this reason. [2] the sheet subtitle printed the internal rack id (R-TEST-1) instead of the label the tech reads off the cabinet (C12-R07); now uses rack.rackId. Also self-caught pre-push: phdock_activeIdx shipped dead (now wired - it seeds the landing phase to in_progress>blocked>first-incomplete, so the sheet "opens scrolled to the active phase" per the queue, and a chip tap overrides + persists as last-viewed); and three comments named a deploy_phaseCardsHtml function that the IIFE approach never created (comment rot, corrected). DEVIATION 1 (reported, deliberate): the queue's chip legend says "done teal"; --teal is NOT a root token (only scoped to #boot and #forge3d-sheet) and the cards paint complete with --green - a chip disagreeing with the card it opens is a field defect, so chips mirror the card colours exactly (done=--green). DEVIATION 2: the queue's premise "the cards render inline on the rack WORK view AND the SET UP SITE flow" is half-wrong (S43) - SET UP SITE (:19320 -> cmd_route('profile') -> siteProfile_showEditor) has NO phase-card markup, so that VERIFY line is trivially true there; ONE renderer/ONE call site means re-homing covers every surface by construction. Owner: confirm which surface you call SET UP SITE. NO rAF: the landing scroll runs synchronously after a forced reflow - rAF does not fire while the page is hidden and would silently skip the scroll (an earlier double-rAF attempt was built on a WRONG diagnosis; the tab was simply backgrounded - document.hidden=true - and the correction was verified, not assumed). LEGACY: ?legacy=1 proven byte-identical LIVE - redesign_isOn()=false, host=#ops-content, 5 cards inline with all phase buttons, 0 cards in the sheet, dock+sheet display:none, phdock_render called 0 times, and a simulated #ta-sheet body-lock SURVIVED phdock_leave (phdock_close only restores overflow if IT took the lock - rule 7). RACK LOCK: untouched - no geometry/materials/lighting/camera/floor/reflection/boot. Gates: node --check 4/0; CSS 12 blocks balanced; CRLF uniform (0 bare-LF); three-stamp .267->.268. John iPhone gate: [1] WORK rack detail: NO phase cards in the scroll; dock sits above the LOG bar; nothing covered; bottom nav still thumb-reachable. [2] Tap each chip -> sheet opens ON that phase; START/GATED/OVERRIDE/checklist drawers all work as before. [3] Drag-down, X and scrim-tap all dismiss; the page does not scroll behind the sheet; a checklist drag does not dismiss it. [4] Tap a phase action (START/BLOCK/COMPLETE) with the sheet open -> the card AND the dock chip update, sheet stays open. [5] Tap COMMAND in the bottom nav with the sheet open -> the sheet closes and the page scrolls normally (this was bug [1]). [6] SUCCESS CRITERION (owner's words): "the U-map is clean and has more room to look the way it should be." [7] ?legacy=1: phase cards still inline, unchanged.
